Bankers London Joint 8tock Bank, Ltd. | Auditors Chatteris, Nichols & Co. Secretary and Offices William Milne, Dashwood House, E.C. Telejrrams: Versifier, London. This company was registered November 18th, 1893, and acquired various properties in Rhodesia, including a grant of 100,000 acres in January, 1894. from tie British South Africa Co. In 1895 the company purchased for 39,000 fullypaid shares the undertakings of the Copenhagen Mashonaland Co., Ltd., the Northumberland Mining Syndicate, Ltd., and the De Beers Prospecting Syndicate. The consulting engineers, R. Williams & Co., were entitled to 10 per cent, of the company s profits, but this right has been extinguished by an allotment of 12,250 fally-paid shares. In 1898 the undertaking of the Selukwe Consolidated, Ltd. M oil. for 1900 , was purchased for £54,000 in fully-paid shares. Properties at October 31st, 1909 : 387,339 acres of freehold land in Rhodesia, three townstands in Bulawayo, and four in Victoria. Mashonaland ; also various shares and debentures. The authorised capital is £200,000 see below , in 200,000 shares of £1 each ; 194,051 shares are issued and fully paid. The capital, originally £12,000, was increased in February, 1894, to £40,000 ; in August, 1894, to £50,000 ; in April, 1895, to £150,000 ; a id in September, 1898, to present amount. The capital is be reduced to £100,000 in 10s. shares, by writing 10s. off each share. A bonus dividend of 25 per cent, was paid in November, 1895, for the period ending October 31st, 1895. The accounts for year ended October 31st, 1909, submitted December 20th, 1909, showed a profit of £240, reducing debit brought forward to £6,651 ; cash £12.422, debtor £2,029, investments £29,032 ; creditors £46 ; property account £143,180 owing to depreciation in share values and claims abandoned, the value was estimated at £51,442 . Transfer form, common; fee, 2g. 6d. The 26th April, 1899, was appointed ettling day in shares Nos. 1 to 189,603; August 30th, 1899, in Nos. 191,768 to 192,767 and 189,604 to 189,989 ; and January 25th, 1900, in Nos. 189,990 to 190,028 and 192,768 to 195,767. This company was registered in the Transvaal August 10th, 1903, and THE MINING MANVAl. WEST HAND CONSOLIDATED acquired 726 reef-bearing claims, and 3 water- rights, situate on farms Laipaards Vlei No. 8, Rietvlei No. 113, and Uitvalfontein No. 2, West Rand, Transvaal, for merly owned by the General Mining and Finance Corporation, Ltd., S. Neumann & Co., A. Goerz & Co., Ltd., Weston Synd., Ltd. see vol. for 1905 , and others ; also 216,696 shares in West Rand Mines, Ltd., at par, and 261,681 Violet Con solidated shares, at about £2 each. The total purchase consideration for claims and shares was £160,893 cash and 1,175,964 ordinary and 25 000 deferred fullypaid shares. In April, 1907, the adjoining undertakings of the Violet Consolidated Gold Mining Co., Ltd. owning 683 claims and 60-stamp mill and cyanide plant , and West Rand Mines, Ltd. owning 669 claims and50-stamp mill , were acquired as from December 31st, 1906, for 553,460 fully-paid shares, payable 270,156 to the Violet Co. and 283,303 to the West Rand Mines, the company cancelling its holding of shares in those companies. The property now comprises 1,907 claims on the main reef series together with water rights, Ac, of which 1,785 claims are in one compact block. See plan. A plant of 60 stamps and three tube mills capable of crushing 18,000 tons per month are being erected, and will form the nucleus of a much larger equipment to be eventually put up as and when the moie extended development of the property justifies. There are at present about . GS5 WEST HAND CONSOLIDATED 800 000 tons of payable ore developed, and crushing is expected to commence in July, 1908. For crashings see table at commencement of volume. The authorised capital is £2,025,000, in 2,000,000 ordinary and 25,000 deferred shares of £1 each ; 1,979,424 ordinary and all the deferred shares are issued and fully paid, the deferred and 1,175,964 ordinary being vendors , 250,000 ordinary subscribed at £2 per share and 553,460 issued to acquire the Violet and West Band Mines Cos. The capital was increased from £1,525,000 in May, 1907. The deferred are entitled to 25 per cent, of all profits after an aggregate dividend of 100 per cent, has been paid on the ordinary, also to subscribe for one-fourth of any future share issued ; the West Band Trust, Ltd., holds all the deferred shares. In January, 1905, 500,000 fully-paid ordinary shares were offered for sale to the public by the General Mining and Finance Corpn., Ltd., at £2 2s. 6d. per share, , issue over-subscribed . The directors have power to inorease the capital to £2,275,000. The accounts for year to December 31st, 1905, submitted in Johannesburg May 1st, 1906, showed cash on deposit at call £300,000, on current account £3,649, shareholdings at cost £814,511 ; creditors £4,593, share preminm account £250,000. The cash balances at December 1st, 1907, amounted to £276,407. A share register is kept in London. Transfer form, common ; fee, 2s. 6d. per 100 shares or part. Share warrants to bearer may be had at a fee of 2s. 6d. per 100 shares or part thereof and Is. per warrant ; fee for reconversion 2s. 6d. per 100 shares or part. The 8th March, 1905, was appointed settling day in ordinary shares Nob. 25,001 to 1,450,964 and July 11th, 1907, in Nos. 1 ,450,965 to 2,004,424 Prices, 1906 : highest, 1 j ; lowest, | ; 1907 : highest, |£ ; lowest, -ft. This company was registered in the Transvaal October 21st, 1895, as the Van der Hoff s Estate and Mining Co., Ltd., and owns the south-eastern portion of the freehold farm Witpoortje, No. 44, comprising 816 morgen, 527 square roods, with mynpacht of 100 morgen, and werf 30 morgen, Krugersdorp district, Trans vaal. The name was changed in 1898. The company derives a revenue from claim licences. The authorised capital is £25,000, in 25,000 shares of £1 each ; all the shares are issued and fully paid. The capital, formerly £125,000, was reduced in 1898, shareholders receiving £1 cash and one new share for every five held. Dividends declared : 10 per oent. in July, 1899 ; 5 per cent, paid August 31st, 1903 ; 10 per cent. January, 1904 ; 5 per cent. December, 1904 ; 5 per cent. Decem ber 31st, 1906. The accounts for year ended December 31st, 1905, submitted in Johannesburg March 27th, 1906, showed a profit of £935, including £3 balance forward. The accounts to December 31st, 1906, submitted in Johannesburg March 26th, 1907, showed profit and loss credit balance increased to £1,641 1 dividend absorbed £1,250, leaving £391 oarried forward. This company was registered in Edinburgh July 14th, 1876, as the Clyde Coil Co., Ltd., to carry on coal mining operations at Hamilton, in Scotland. In 1892 the company acquired the business and collieries of John Wilson, when the name of the company was changed. In 1894 the company acquired the lease of a coalfield on the estate of Glencraig, near Lochgelly, Fifeshire. The collieries are known as Shawfield, Brownlee, Law, GiUhead, DouglM Park, E. Parkhead, Clyde, Wellhall, and Glencraig Fife , covering 3,350 acres; also Croashill Estates, adjoining Glencraig, acquired in 1902. The company ha also acquired the lease of the Saline and Outh coalfields, Fifeshire, about 8,1W acres, near the new Bosyth Naval Base ; a railway three miles long has been constructed to develop this property. Plant is being erected and on the Onti portion boring operations are in progress. The authorised capital is £230,000, in 4,000 seven per cent, cumulative fir preference and 1,000 seven per cent, cumulative second preference shares of £M each and 60,000 ordinary shares of £3 each ; all the shares are issued, 1,791 first preference and 60,000 ordinary being fully paid, and 2,209 first preference and all the second preference having £5 called, making the paid-up capital £213.955. The capital, originally £150,000, subsequently inoreased to £190,000, in preference and ordinary shares, was reduced in 1884 to £85,000 by writing off £7 per ordinary share, and the preference dividend was reduced from 10 to 7 per cent. In 1898 the capital was increased to £170,000 by creation of the second preference and 25,000 new ordinary shares. The second preference were allotted to shareholders at par, and the new ordinary at £2 premium. In 1894 the capital was increased to present amount by creation and issue of 20,000 ne w ordinary shares to share- . . . miscellaneous companies. 1061 WILSONS AND CLYDE COAL oontlnued. holders. The consideration was £250,000, paid £120,160 in cash, £29,840 in fully-paid preference, and £100,000 in fully-paid ordinary shares. The oompany was promoted by the vendors, the Windsor Steam Coal Co., Ltd., who guaranteed the preference dividend for two years. Sinking is completed, and the colliery is working. The authorised capital is £275,000, in 12,500 six per cent, cumulative preference with priority for capital , and 15,000 ordinary shares of £10 each ; all the preference and 10,000 ordinary shares are issued and fully paid, the ordinary and 2,984 preference being issued credited as fully paid. There are £125,000 five per cent, registered first mortgage debentures in bonds of £100, secured by trust deed, constituting a floating charge on the undertaking and redeemable after January 1st, 1909, by annual or other drawings at 106 per cent. ; interest is payable on January 1st and July 1st; the first surplus profits, after payment of preferential dividend, are to be applied as to 3 per cent, on the amount of first debentures towards forming a fund for their redemption, and the dividend on the ordinary shares is limited to 10 per cent., until a fund amounting to £60,000 has been formed and invested in the name of the trustee to debenture-holders. This company was registered in the Transvaal in March, 1906, to acquire the freehold farm 8nyman s Drift No. 4, area 3,028 morgon, situate in the Zontpansberg distriot of the Transvaal. The consideration was £17,500 in fullypaid shares. The African and European Investment Co., Ltd., was interested in the sale. A 10-stamp mill has been erected and crushing commenced Aug., 1907. The authorised capital is £25,000, in 25,000 shares of £1 each ; 22,500 shares are issued and fully paid including vendors . SOCIETA EREYTRHITRREAEAGOLPDERMINLINEG CMOIMNPIEANRYE . D ORO Direotors. P.O.T., 8767 Central. This company was registered in the Italian colony of Erythrea on June 24th, 1900, to take over a mining concession granted by the Italian Government over lands situate in Erythrea, North Abyssinia. The property consists of three blocks, aggregate area of 117 square miles, on the Asmara Plateau, including the township of Asmara capital of the colony , distant about 70 miles from the port of Massowah. Development is proceeding, and supplies of ore are being opened up. The authorised capital is 2,000,000 lire £80,000 , in 80,000 shares to bearer of 25 lire £1 each ; all the shares are issued and fully paid. In 1906 the shares were written down to 12 5 lire each, and 40,000 new shares of 25 lire each were offered to shareholders at the rate of one new for every two old shares held ; the uld shares were then converted into shares of 25 lire each, shareholders receiving one for every two held. Accounts for year endod December 31st, 1905, submitted in Borne May, 1906, showed cash 3,514 liro ; on current account 192,407 francs, Government bonds 98,950 lire j debtors 10,547 lire ; croditors 19,014 liro ; reserve fund 84,153 lire. No later accounts received. somaTjlanIO S This company was registered June 8th, 1905 see vol. for 1906 . On September 23rd, 1907, it was resolved to wind up voluntarily; liquidator, H. A. H. SOOWIN CONCESSIONS This company was registered on August 28th, 1901, to acquire, by agreement with the Ashanti Three Rivers Synd., Ltd., the Soowin concession, about 144 square miles, between Broomassie and Tarkwa.Wassau district, Gold Coast Colony. The consideration was £75,000, paid £10,000 in cash and £65,000 in fully -paid shares. Other properties known as Nyinaa Bippo and Bogydayau have been purchased for £15,000 in fully-paid shares. Amalgamation with other companies is under consideration. The authorised capital is £100,000, in 100,000 shares of £1 each ; 90,000 shares are issued and fully paid, including 65,000 vendors . No accounts yet issued. Transfer form, common ; fee, 2s. 6d. The 22nd May, 1902, was appointed a settling day in shares Nos. 50,001 to 75,000. SOUD AN DEVELO PM ENT AND EXPLORATION COMPANY, LIMITED. Hall, Khedive Avenue. Solicitors Hollams, Sons, Coward & Hawksley. Bankers Union of London & Smiths Bank. Limited. | Auditors Cooper Brothers & Co. Local Secretary and Offices Th. Nossif, Rue Daram alii. Box 79 , Cairo. Paris Office 7, Rue Meyerbeer. Secretary and Offices Thomas Day, 31, Clement s Lane, E.C. N.T., 986 Avenue. This company was registered on March 20th, 1900, and acquired a concession from the Soudan Government for a service of steamers and barges, under guarantee from the Government, to trade on the Blue and White Niles. These boats now run a regular service, and a trading business is also being established. The business of the company has been practically taken over by the New Egyptian Co., Ltd., by an exohange of shares. The authorised capital is £100,000, in 97,500 ordinary shares of £1 each and 100,000 deferred shares of 6d. each ; 67,500 ordinary and all the deferred shares are issued and fully paid. The deferred shares are entitled to one-half the surpluB profits in each year, after a dividend of 6 per cent, has been paid on the ordinary shares for Buch year. There are £50,000 five per cent, navigation bonds debentures authorised, of which £32,500 are issued. The accounts for year to October 31st, 1906, submitted on October 28th, 1907, showed £14,861 to debit of income and expenditure account, an inorease of £3,753 on the year. This company was registered in the Transvaal June 9th, 1902, and acquired from the Apex, Chimes Mines, and other companies, 420 gold claims on farm Benoni, Eastern Witwatersrand, Transvaal. The consideration was 392,499 fully-paid shares. The main reef series has been struck by borehole at 2,195ft. to 2,320ft. depth. Operations are suspended pending raising of further working capital. . 567 VAN ETN DEEP oontinued. The authorised capital is £785,000, in 785,000 shares of £1 each ; 692,499 shares are issued, 392,499 vendors shares being fully-paid, and 200,000 subscribed at par for working capital, tho guarantors receiving an option on the reserve shares at £2 each. The accounts for year ended April 80th, 1907, submitted in Johannesburg September 6th, 1907, showed cash £5,474, uncalled capital £6,000 since called up | creditors £4,453. A share register is kept in London. Transfer form, common; fee, 2s. 6d. per deed of 100 shares or part. VAN RYN GOL nvTTNES ESTATE. LIMITED. Directors Qualification, £250 . Frank A. Gillam, Chairman. Bankers London & Westminster Bank, Limited ; Dresdner Bank. Auditors Deloitte, Plender, Griffiths & Co. Ziooal Secretary and Offices J. V. Blinkhorn, F.S.A.A., General Mining Buildings, Marshall Square, Box 1173, Johannesburg, Transvaal. Berlin Agents General Mining & Finance Corporation, Limited, 61, Markgrafenstrasse.W. Paris Agents General Mining & Finance Corporation, Limited, 6, Rue Meyerbeer. Seoretary and Head Offices Stuart James Hogg, 257, Winchester House, B.C. Telegrams : Vansire. London. N.T., 854 Wall. This company was registered June 22nd, 1894, and is a reconstruction of the Van Ryn Estate and Gold Mining Co., Ltd. see vol. for 1896 . The property now owned is situated on the farm Vlakfontein No. 442, in the Eastern district of the Witwatersrand Gold Fields, and is 7,944 acres in extent, in addition to an unproclaimed area of 534 morgen recently acquired. The mining area on the Van Eyn reef, the ore body which is being worked, consists of some 136 intact reef claims after allowing for the ground exhausted, while the company s waterrights cover 351$ acres. Crushing commenced in February, 1892, and in October, 1896, a new 80-stamp mill and cyanide plant were started. Another 80-stamp mill was acquired with the Van Ryn West property, making 160 stamps. A new slimes plant has been added and four tube mills are being erected. Crushing was suspended in October, 1899, and during the war the machinery was damaged by the Boers, but this has been replaced, and crushing was resumed in June, 1903. During 1906-7, 266,940 tons yielded gold value £496,686 or 37s. 16d. per ton at a cost of 21s. 9-2d. and a profit of 15s. 4-4d. per ton ; during 1907-8 to December, 1907 , 135,642 tons crushed yielded gold value £238,472 and a profit of £101,659. For crushings see tablex at commencement of volume. Ore reserves at June, 1906, 627,880 tons, of whioh 433,580 tons have a value exceeding 8dwts. ; at June, 1907, 1,119,600 tons average value 8 74dwts. The authorised capital is £500,000, in 500,000 shares of £1 each; all the shares are issued and fully paid. The capital was formerly £160,000, one fullypaid share being given for evory two shares of old company ; in 1895 the capital was increased to £200,000, 10,000 shares being issued to shareholders at £6 each, 10,000 taken up at £6 each under an option, and 20,000 issued credited as fully paid in payment for the undertaking of the Van Ryn North Co. In 1898 the capital was increased to £400,000, 50,000 shares being issued in payment for the Van Eyn West undertaking and the remainder being taken up at 30s. each. The capital was inoreased to present amount in November, 1901 ; the 100,000 new shares were issued to shareholders in February, 1902, at £2 15s. Dividends paid : 4s. per share on old capital January 15th, 1896 ; 2s. January 18th, 1905 ; 2s. July 10th, 1905 ; 2s. January 10th, 1906 ; 2s. July 10th, 1906 ; 2s. January 11th, 1907 ; 4s. July 10th, 1907 ; 3s. January 10th, W08. This company was registered June 1st, 1900, to acquire from the West Le Roi Mining Co., Ltd., the Josie, Poorman, Annie and Annie Fractions, Rocking ham and Number One mineral claims, formerly called West Le Roi, and now known as Le Roi No. 2 Group, embracing 72 acres in the mineral zone of Ross land, British Columbia. The group immediately adjoins the Le Roi, War Eagle and Centre Star mines, and is distant about half-a-mile from the Rossland stations of the two trans-continental railways, the Canadian Pacific and the Great Northern. The consideration was £550,000, in cash. Tho ore contains gold, silver, and copper ; a concentrating plant is at work and ore shipments are in progress. Further claims in the Rossland and Ymir districts were acquired in 1905 and an option on a silver-lead property at Silverton in 1906. Development

This company was registered December 10th, 1906, to acquire the undertaking of a oompany of similar name registered June 19th, 1901 see vol. for 1907 . The property comprises two leases of tin, copper and arsenical mundio mines, area 100 acres, at Peter Tavy, Devonshire. The consideration was £14,000 payable £9,000 in fully-paid shares and £5,000 in six per oent. debentures. Development work is in progress. The authorised capital is £26,000, in 25,000 shares of £1 each ; 20,050 shares are issued, 20,000 being fully paid and 50 having 10s. paid. In September, 1907, 6,000 shares were offered to shareholders at par. fBE M1NIXQ MAXVAL. DEVONSHIRE MINES, LIMITED. Directors Qualification, 1 0shares . Dudley J. C. Bush, J. P. I L. B. Atkinson. This company was registered December 5tb, 1906, to acquire and work the North Hoc Lead Mine, on the banks of the River Taniar, Devonshire, the consideration was £6,000 in fully-paid shares. The authorised capital is £12,000, in 12,000 shares of £1 each. In December, 1906, 6,000 shares were offered for subscription, payable 2s. 6d. on appl cation, 2s. 6d. on allotment, and the balance in calls not exceeding 5s. at intervals of not less than two months. DHARWAR GOLD MINES. LIMITED. Directors Qualification, £100 . Capt. W. Bell McTaggart, Chairman. H. D. H. Ferguason, J. P. John Taylor, M.Inst.C.E. | Robert Taylor. | F. Shaw Kennedy, J.P. Managers John Taylor and Sons. Superintendent Robert Collins, Kabulayatkatti P.O., Dharwar District, Bombay Presidency. Bankers Union Bank of Scotland, Limited. I Solicitors Francis and Johnson. Auditors Turquaod, Youngs and Co. Secretary and Offices John Ponsford, 5-6, Queen Street Place, E.C. N.T., 8 9 Bank. This oompany was registered on January 16th, 1902, to acquire the option to purchase four mining properties on the Kabligatti line of reefs, Gadag or Dambal Goldfield, province of Dharwar, Bombay Presidency, the purchase consideration to be £4,000 in cash and one-third of the capital, exclusive of working capital., in fully-paid shares of the new company to be formed to take over the properties. Additional properties in the same district hare been acquired. The option on the original properties has been exercised, and the new company, the Dharwar Reefs Co., Ltd., has been floated. The company also granted an option for three years from January, 1905, over Block No. 19 160 aores to the Dharwar Reefs Co., Ltd., the purchase price to be 25,000 fully-paid shares; this option was exercised in November, 1906. An option for three years from March 1st, 1907, has been granted to the Mysore Gold Mining Co., Ltd., over Block 22a, area 160 acres, the consideration to be £5,000 cash and £25,000 in cish or shares. The company now holds Government mining leases over 1,902 acres, prospecting licenses over 2,114 acres and an option until June 30th, 1909, over 3,333 acres. The Hosur, Attikatti and Sankadodak properties are being developed. The authorised capital is £45,000, in 45,000 shares of £1 each ; all the shares are issued, 30,000 being fully paid and 15,000 issued at 10s. premium having 10s. called up, the balance being pavable in calls of 5b. on February Is , 1908, May 1st, 1908, August 1st, 1908, and November 2nd, 1908. In January, 1902, 30,000 shares were offered at par to shareholders of the Nundydroog Co., Ltd., and in June, 1907, the capital was increased from £30,000 to present amount, the new shares being offered to shareholders at 30s. each at the rate of one for every two held. Dividends paid : 1904-6, 133 per cent. June 2nd, 1905, by distribution of 40,000 shares of the Dharwar Reefs Co., Ltd., being four for every three shares held ; 1905-6, nil; 1906-7,40 per cent.; June 27th, 1907, by distribution of 12,000 Dharwar Reefs shares, being two for every five shares held. The accounts for year ended March 31st, 1906, submitted June 27th, 1906, showed a debit of £5,309, from whioh £2,433 credit balance brought forward was deduoted, leaving a sum of £2.876 to debit of profit and loss account. The accounts for year ended March 31st, 1907, submitted June 24th, 1907, showed £26,006 to credit of profit and loss account; deducting £2,876 debit brought forward, £7,914 mining and general expenditure and £211 depreciation left £15,004; dividend June, 1907 Bnd directors and managers percentage absorbed £12,600, leaving £2,404 credit carried forward ; assets £16,030, including 4,989 fully-paid and 997 5s. paid Dharwar Reefs shares ; liabilities £1,311. This company was registered on October 23th, 1904, to acquire two leases of auriferous properties in the Taluq of Gadag, distriot of Dharwar, Bombay Presidency, granted by the Seoretary of State for India. One lease is for 30 years from August 4th, 1902, of mining blocks No. 1, Nabapoor, 160 acres, No. 2, Kabulayatkatti orKabligatti , 160 acres, and No. 3, Sortur, 160 acres, subject to half-yearly rental of 240 rupees, surface rent of 8 annas per acre, and royalty of 7 per cent, of net profits from gold extracted above the amount of half-yearly rental ; the other lease is for 30 years from January pt, 1904, of mining Block No. 4, Attikatti and Sortur, 160 aores, subject to half-yearly rental of 80 rnpees, and surface rent and royalty as above. The consideration was £105,000, paid £9,000 in cash and £96,000 in fully-pai d shares. The company was promoted by the vendors, the Dharwar Gold Mines, Ltd. ; the original lessee of the property, H. D. Puzey, received £4,000 caBh and £33,334 in shares out of the consideration. In 1906 the company acquired from the vendor company Block No. 19 160 acres , situate immediately to the east of the Kabligatti property, for 25,000 fully-paid shares. An option for three years from March 1st, 1907, over Block No. 3 has been granted to the Mysore Gold Mining Co., Ltd., the consideration for option to be £5,000. Bhould the option be exercised, the purchase consideration to be £70,000, payable £30,000 in cash, including the £5,000 for option, and £40,000 either in fully-paid shares of the purchasing company, to be taken at their selling price on the date the option is exercised, or by payment in cash, or by the allotment of fully-paid shares in any new company formed for the purpose of acquiring and working the property, the capital of such new company not to exceed £250,000, in shares of £1 each, of which 75,000 shall be working capital. The option-holders also undertake to expend in development, work, should they retain the option for the fnll period of three years, the sum of at least £24,000. Development work is in progress chiefly on the Kabligatti Block and a 15-stamp battery and cyanide plant are erected ; crushing commenced February 6th, 1907. For croshings see table at commencement of volume. Ore reserves at December 31st, 1906, 17,686 tons. The authorised capital is £235,000, in 235,000 shares of £1 each ; all the shares are issued, and fully paid. In November, 1904, 90,000 shares were offered for public subscription. The capital was increased from £175,000 in November, 1906, 35,000 shares being offered to shareholders at par at the rate of the one for every five held ; the final call was 5s., due October 5tti, 1907.
